clues.clue: This live-music city is the capital of Texas.
This city on the American River is California's capital.
This upstate city, not the largest one, is the capital of New York.
This Panhandle city is the capital of Florida.
This city, sharing a witch-trial name, is the capital of Oregon.
Arizona's capital shares its name with a mythical bird reborn from ashes.
Wisconsin's capital is named for the fourth U.S. president.
Minnesota's capital, named for an apostle, is the twin city to Minneapolis.
Indiana's capital tacks the Greek word for 'city' onto the state's name.
Colorado's 'Mile-High' capital sits at roughly 5,280 feet of elevation.
An Alaska statute is often cited for banning waking this hibernating animal to take a photo.
An 1897 Indiana bill nearly legally redefined this circle-ratio constant to 3.2.
Carmel, California once required a permit to wear these tall shoes on its sloped streets.
Gainesville, Georgia 'requires' that this fried bird be eaten only with one's hands.
A reputed Kansas rule forbids topping cherry pie on Sundays with this cold dairy treat.
This southeastern state is nicknamed 'The Sunshine State.'
This large state is 'The Lone Star State.'
This state's skeptical nickname is 'The Show-Me State.'
This northern state is the 'Land of 10,000 Lakes.'
This island state is 'The Aloha State.'
This northern state is by far the largest in the U.S. by land area.
This New England state is the smallest in the country by area.
This New England state is the only one bordering just a single other state.
Tennessee and this 'Show-Me' state each touch eight neighboring states.
Four states meet at the single point known by this geographic name.

final.clue: This purpose-built inland city, not Sydney or Melbourne, is the capital of Australia.
